rulings.Case Title: TANDBERG,INC. A DELAWARE CORPORATION VS. MARGARET A SELTZER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION And Notice Of Motion For Award Of Appelate Attorneys Fees And Costs Pursuant To Ccp 425.16(C)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 20, 2010, line 1, CROSS DEFENDANT WILLIAM GWIRE Motion For Award of Appellate Attorney Fees and Costs Pursuant to CCP 425.16(C. Motion for award of appellate attorney fees and costs granted. Attorney fees reasonable and necessary, and the hourly fee claimed is within the range of fees typically charged by attorneys in the community. Gwire to recover $58,415 as reasonable fees for the appeal (166.9 hours at $350 per hour) and $9,450 for this motion (27 hours at $350 per hour) plus costs in the amount of $1,241.90. Request for additonal discovery denied as unnecessary. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID C. ANDERSON VS. BLAIR ADAMS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 20, 2010, line 2, DEFENDANT PETER FLICKINGER Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings. Motion granted without leave to amend as to 2nd, 3rd, 4th, and 6th causes of action. Causes of action are barred by the litigation privilege. Motion denied as to the 1st, 7th, and 8th causes of action that are not tort-based but instead are contract-based claims. Motion denied as to the 5th cause of action for civil conspiracy which is proper under CC section 1714.10. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JEFF BREIBART VS. SAN FRANCISCO CITY & COUNTY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 20, 2010, line 6, DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, MATTHEW ROTHSCHILD Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings. Motion for judgment on the pleadings as to the 2nd and 3rd causes of action granted without leave to amend, no reasonable probability that defects can be cured by amendment. The city has immunity pursuant to Government Code section 815(a). Even without immunity plaintiff has not established that the City owed him a duty to identify the dog and owner with due care. It was not foreseeable that the failure to locate the video would result in plaintiff having to get rabies shots. Plaintiff had to get rabies shots because he was bitten by a dog, not because MUNI failed to locate a video. Too many assumptions are made to support a cause of action, such as the location of the video would have led to the identification of the dog within ten days and that the dog did not have rabies. Plaintiff fails to establish that Rothschild owed him a duty. It is not foreseeable that an inaccurate email by Rothschild would cause plaintiff to have to get rabies shots. As to the 2nd and 3rd causes of action, no showing that any act or omission by CCSF or any of its employees was the proximate cause of plaintiff's damages (rabies shots). =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: NORTEK PRODUCTS (TAICHANG) LIMITED, et al VS. IP CLEANING S.P.A.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Lack Of Jurisdiction;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 20, 2010, line 14, DEFENDANT IP CLEANING S.P.A. Motion to Dismiss for Lack of Jurisdiction. Motion granted. Plaintiff fails to establish that this court has personal jurisdiction over defendant in either form, general or specific. Defendant does not have substantial, systematic and continuous ties to California to support general jurisdiction. Defendant is a foreign corporation as are all the parties. No showing that defendant has property, offices, facilities, employees, or bank accounts in California, or that defendant maintains marketing efforts in California. No specific jurisdiction: the contract was negotiated outside of California and provides all disputes are to be governed by Italian law and any unresolved controversy shall be exclusively settled by the Court of Milan. Defendant would likely incur significant expense in defending this suit in California. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: NANCY D. WOLD VS. PAUL FINANCIAL, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 20, 2010, line 15, DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA RECONVEYANCE COMPANY DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend. As to each cause of action, Plaintiff must specifically state its number, its nature, and the party or parties to whom it is directed. It is not possible to identify from the face of the complaint which allegations pertain to which defendant. Plaintiff must allege facts to support each cause of action. =(302/CWW)
